zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell於1350年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾爾維爾時，他的父親Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell31歲，母親Basile Eve Culting28歲。他於1372年與Cathrine DeCharnes在英國諾廷加姆爾爾維爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1384年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾被列為ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia。他去世於1401年在他的家鄉享年51歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾爾維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell于1350年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾爾維爾时，他的父亲Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell31岁，母亲Basile Eve Culting28岁。他于1372年与Cathrine DeCharnes在英国諾廷加姆爾爾維爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1384年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾被列为ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia。他去世于1401年在他的家乡享年51岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾爾維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.
Italian (Veneto): variant of   Masone  .
Altered form of French   Masson  .

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.
Scottish: variant of   Mason   ‘mason’.
Scottish: variant of   Meston  .

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

variants.name: Meason
meanings: English: variant of   Mason  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell was born in 1350, in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell, was 31 and his mother, Basile Eve Culting, was 28. He married Cathrine DeCharnes in 1372, in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. In 1384, at the age of 34, his occupation is listed as ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1401, in his hometown, at the age of 51, and was buried in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell est né en 1350, à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Basile Eve Culting, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Cathrine DeCharnes en 1372, à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. En 1384, à l’âge de 34 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1401, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell 1350 in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell 31 und seine Mutter Basile Eve Culting 28. Er hat 1372 in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England Cathrine DeCharnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. 1384, als er 34 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb 1401 im Alter von 51 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell è nato il nel 1350 nel Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, il suo padre, Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Basile Eve Culting, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Cathrine DeCharnes nel 1372, nel Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Nel 1384, all'età di 34 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia nel Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1401, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwellが1350年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルで生まれた時，父Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwellは31歳，母Basile Eve Cultingは28歳であった。1372年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルでCathrine DeCharnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。1384年，34歳の時，イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでの職業はocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesiaと記録されている。彼は1401年に故郷で51歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell(이)가 1350년 영국 노틴그함실르 치웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Basile Eve Culting(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1372년 영국 노틴그함실르 치웰에서 Cathrine DeCharnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1384년, 34세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia이었다. 그는 1401년 그의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 치웰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell nasceu em 1350, em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Basile Eve Culting, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Cathrine DeCharnes em 1372, em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Em 1384, com 34 anos, sua profissão aparece como ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1401, em sua cidade natal, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell родился в 1350 г. в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Basile Eve Culting, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Cathrine DeCharnes в 1372 г. в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1384 году, в возрасте 34 лет,  он работал по профессии ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1401 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell nació en 1350, en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord Richard "Cementorius" Mason of Chillwell, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Basile Eve Culting, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Cathrine DeCharnes en 1372, en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. En 1384, a la edad de 34 años, su ocupación registrada era ocupación: la iglesia de san juan bautista es una iglesia anglicana en beeston,  vicario de la iglesia en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en 1401, en su pueblo, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
